---
title: "2,3-DPG and pH in stored RBC"
subtitle: "Reproducible analysis — *Journal of Neurotrauma* submission"
author:
  - name: Juan Sahuquillo
  - name: Marta Peris
date: "2026-02-02"
date-modified: "2026-06-16"
abstract: |
  The efficacy of red blood cell (RBC) transfusions depends on oxygen
  delivery, which deteriorates during storage due to the "storage lesion,"
  notably 2,3-diphosphoglycerate (2,3-DPG) depletion. We characterized
  the temporal dynamics of 2,3-DPG depletion and metabolic acidification
  in 24 leukodepleted packed RBC units stored under standard conditions
  from day 3 to 42. 2,3-DPG concentrations (µmol/g Hb) were quantified
  using liquid chromatography–mass spectrometry (LC–MS) with isotopically
  labeled internal standards, alongside parallel pH measurements. Baseline
  (day 3) 2,3-DPG levels showed substantial inter-unit variability (median
  4.8 µmol/g Hb; range 0.8–14.7). A rapid decline occurred across all
  units, dropping to ~27% of baseline by day 7, >90% by day 14, and
  falling below the detection limit after day 17. Mixed-effects modeling
  confirmed a strong negative association between storage duration and
  2,3-DPG (β = −0.298 µmol/g Hb/day; p < 0.001). Concurrently, pH
  decreased progressively from 7.06 ± 0.06 to 6.40 ± 0.04 (p < 0.001).
  In conclusion, 2,3-DPG depletion is an early, rapid phenomenon linked
  to acidification that impairs oxygen unloading. Preserving 2,3-DPG is
  a critical, modifiable target for improving stored RBC quality,
  suggesting that storage duration alone may be an inadequate surrogate
  for RBC metabolic quality.
editor: visual
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    self-contained: true
---

# Settings for R session

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

Sys.setenv(LANG = "en")
graphics.off()
options(scipen=999, digits=4)
```

# Load libraries

```{r}
#| label: packages
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(broom)
library(emmeans)
library(flextable)
library(ggthemes)
library(here)
library(janitor)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(performance)
library(psych)
library(readxl)
library(rstatix)
library(see) # Required for the visual plotting features
library(tidyverse)
library(writexl)
```

# Import 2,3_DPG dataset

```{r}
#| label: import-dpg
#| message: false

# Establish the file path
path <- here("data", "2,3-dpg.xlsx")
path
readxl::excel_sheets(path)
import <- readxl::read_excel(
  path = path, 
  sheet = "2,3-DPG(umol-gHb)", 
  col_types = "text"
)
# Preview the first 6 rows of the imported matrix
head(import)

# Change first column name
colnames(import)[1] <-"Day"

# Fix variable names
mydata <- import |> 
  janitor::clean_names()

mydata <- mydata |> 
  mutate(across(-c(day), ~ as.numeric(.)))

head(mydata)
names(mydata)
glimpse (mydata)
```

# Save the final RDS data file

```{r}
saveRDS(mydata, file = here("data", "2_3_DPG.rds"))
```

# Import pH dataset

```{r}
#| label: import-ph
#| message: false
#| eval: false

# Secure the path relative to the project root
ph_path <- here("data", "pH.xlsx")
ph_path
# Audit sheet names
readxl::excel_sheets(ph_path)
import_ph <- read_excel(ph_path, sheet = "pH", col_types = "text")
head(import_ph)

# Change first column name to Day
colnames(import_ph)[1] <-"Day"

# Fix variable names
mydata_ph<-import_ph |> 
  janitor::clean_names()
head(mydata_ph)

mydata_ph <- mydata_ph |> 
  mutate(across(-c(day), ~ as.numeric(.)))

head(mydata_ph)
names(mydata_ph)
glimpse(mydata_ph)
```

# Save the final pH rds.data file

```{r}
saveRDS(mydata_ph, file = here("data", "Stored_blood_pH.rds"))
```
